faqs.q: How quickly could an extension be installed at my Preston home?
faqs.a: Faster than you might expect. Because your extension is built in our Blackburn factory — around half an hour from most Preston postcodes via the M65 and M6 — there's no long wait for trades to become available. The build itself takes as little as four weeks in the factory, and once it arrives, installation on site typically takes under a week. We'll confirm a realistic timeline for your specific project at the design stage, before you commit to anything.

faqs.q: Will I need planning permission in Preston?
faqs.a: Not always. Permitted development rights often cover single-storey rear extensions and garden rooms, which is what most of our Preston projects are — but the rules depend on your property and any previous alterations, so always check with Preston City Council (or South Ribble Borough Council if you're in Penwortham or Longton). We'll talk you through what's likely to apply to your home early in the design process, so there are no surprises later.

faqs.q: My house is on a modern estate — can you actually get an extension in?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes. Access is one of the first things we assess, and it's rarely a problem on the newer estates around Fulwood, Penwortham and Longton, where roads and driveways tend to be reasonably generous. We survey your property before anything is built, plan the delivery route in advance, and coordinate any temporary parking arrangements with you. If there's a genuine access constraint, we'll tell you upfront — not on delivery day.

faqs.q: What do Preston homeowners typically use a modular extension for?
faqs.a: The most popular choice is a bigger kitchen or open-plan kitchen-diner — the room most family homes around Preston are short of. Home offices are a close second, particularly for people who've swapped the daily commute for working from home a few days a week. We also design dedicated kids' playrooms and standalone garden rooms, which suit the larger plots common on estates in Fulwood, Penwortham and Longton particularly well.

heroHeading: Beautiful Modular Extensions, Built for Preston Homes
intro:
Preston is a city of family homes — from the leafy avenues of Fulwood to the estates of Penwortham and Longton — and sooner or later, most of those homes start to feel a room short. A kitchen that can't quite hold everyone at once. A dining table doubling as a desk. A growing family and nowhere for the toys to go.

That's exactly the problem EazyBase was built to solve. We design and install modular home extensions in Preston that are factory-built just down the road in Blackburn — precision-made indoors in as little as four weeks, then delivered and installed at your home in under a week. No months of scaffolding, no building site where your garden used to be.

Whether it's a bigger kitchen, a proper home office, a playroom or a garden room, you get a warm, fully finished space that feels like it was always part of the house — with far less disruption than a traditional build.

localAngles.heading: Room to grow in Fulwood, Penwortham and Longton
localAngles.body: Preston's commuter belt is full of well-built family houses that simply need more space. The newer estates around Fulwood, Penwortham and Longton are particularly well suited to what we do: plots tend to be generous, gardens are a decent size, and there's usually room for a single-storey rear extension or a garden room without sacrificing the outdoor space you bought the house for. Rather than moving — and paying stamp duty for the privilege — many homeowners here find that one well-designed extra room transforms how the whole house works.

localAngles.heading: Planning in and around Preston
localAngles.body: Good news for most of our Preston customers: permitted development rights often cover single-storey rear extensions and garden rooms, meaning a full planning application may not be needed. That said, the rules depend on your particular property — so always check with Preston City Council, or South Ribble Borough Council if your home falls in Penwortham or Longton. We help you work out what applies to your home at the design stage, before anything goes into production, so you can move forward with complete confidence.

localAngles.heading: Why factory-built works so well here
localAngles.body: Our factory is in Blackburn, a straightforward run along the M65 and M6 to almost any Preston address. That proximity matters. Your extension is built indoors, away from Lancashire weather, with every component checked before it leaves the factory floor — then delivered and installed at your home in under a week. Compare that with a traditional build: months of mud, skips and uncertainty. For busy commuter households, keeping the disruption to days rather than seasons is usually the deciding factor.

localAngles.heading: The rooms Preston families actually want
localAngles.body: Every home is different, but the requests we hear from Preston homeowners follow familiar themes. Open-plan kitchen-diners top the list — the heart-of-the-home space so many houses here were built without. Home offices come a close second, as more people split the week between home and the office. And for younger families, a dedicated playroom or a garden room gives everyone breathing space. Whatever you're planning, we design it around your house, your garden and the way your family actually lives.

metaDescription: Factory-built modular home extensions for Preston homes. Designed and made nearby in Blackburn, installed at your home in under a week.
